title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index, 2020
The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

This world map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for 2020 using a continuous color scale ranging from 0 to 0.8, where darker shades of blue represent higher index scores and lighter shades represent lower scores. The highest values, falling in the 0.7 to 0.8 band, are visible across North America, specifically Canada and the United States, as well as Australia, New Zealand, and most of Western and Northern Europe including the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and the Nordic countries. Japan also appears in this top tier. A middle range of development, indicated by medium blue tones corresponding roughly to the 0.4 to 0.6 band, covers much of Latin America, including Brazil, Argentina, and Chile, alongside nations in Eastern Europe and parts of East Asia such as China. Lower index scores, represented by very pale blue or near-white colors in the 0.2 to 0.4 range, dominate the African continent, particularly across the Sahel and Central Africa, as well as parts of South Asia including India and Pakistan. Greenland and a few other territories are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The geographic distribution shows a clear concentration of high index scores in the Global North and Oceania, while the lowest scores cluster in sub-Saharan Africa and parts of Asia. The map presents a gradient of human development achievement that aligns closely with broad regional economic patterns.
